key_insight: In neutron-irradiated tungsten, strain rate and indentation length scale affect nanoindentation response in different ways: strain rate mainly changes thermally activated flow stress, while indentation depth controls plastic-zone size, pile-up, and GND buildup.
engineering_relevance: This decoupled framework helps predict irradiation-hardened tungsten behavior under transient loading, supporting better constitutive models for nuclear and high-heat-flux components.
method: Spherical nanoindentation across strain rates from 3.2e-5 to 3.2e-3 s^-1
AFM and HR-EBSD to measure pile-up, residual strain, and GND distributions
Strain-gradient CPFE with thermally activated slip, GND hardening, irradiation hardening, and softening
evidence_signal: A single calibrated CPFE parameter set was validated against all remaining strain-rate conditions and then used to independently vary strain rate and indentation depth in simulation.
watch_item: The model is calibrated from one experimental condition, so transfer to other irradiation states, temperatures, or microstructures may require re-validation.
paper_title: Decoupling Strain-Rate Sensitivity and Deformation Length Scale Effects in Neutron-Irradiated Tungsten: A Coupled Nano-Indentation, HR-EBSD and Crystal Plasticity Study
